provider: ESPN (site.api.espn.com) — free public API, no key, no quota
authHeaderName: (none — ESPN's site API needs no key or auth)
rateLimitNotes: No published key/auth and no daily quota — effectively unlimited for a personal app, but UNOFFICIAL: the response shape can change without notice, so normalisers are defensive and we cache (Vercel CDN in prod, a disk cache in dev). Be a good citizen: don't hammer it.

endpoints.purpose: All matches (schedule + results + LIVE status) — one date-range call covers the whole tournament
endpoints.responseNotes: events[] each: id (the event id used by /summary), date, season{slug: 'group-stage'|'round-of-32'|… -> stage}, competitions[0].{status.type.state (pre|in|post), competitors[]{homeAway, team.displayName, score, shootoutScore}}. state 'in' gives a live clock + score.

endpoints.purpose: Single match box score + goal/card timeline (radar + match report + fantasy)
endpoints.responseNotes: boxscore.teams[].statistics[] {name,displayValue,value}: possessionPct, totalShots, shotsOnTarget, foulsCommitted, yellowCards, redCards, saves, … (NO xG/xA/key passes -> radar Creativity stays null). keyEvents[] {type.text (Goal/Yellow Card/…), clock.displayValue, team.displayName, participants[0]=scorer, participants[1]=assist, text}. header.competitions[0].competitors[] for final + shootout score.

notes: The app migrated from Highlightly to ESPN to drop the API key + daily quota entirely. The SEED (group results/standings/schedule) is still compiled from public web sources (see fixtures.json/groups.json sources); ESPN supplies the live runtime data and the baked group-stage box scores (scripts/fetch-espn.mjs -> src/data/teamStats.ts). PENDING refinement: penalty-shootout detection for knockout fantasy scoring (ESPN's shootout event shape — verify when the first KO shootout occurs).
